subject: Five things to check before the first nor'easter
preheader: Trees, drainage, garage doors, generators — one weekend walk-around, a quieter winter.
title: Storm-ready: prepping your Cape home for nor'easter season
introHtml: Noreaster season on the Cape runs roughly October through April, and the first big blow usually finds whatever you didn't get to. The good news: most storm damage starts with small, checkable things. Here's the walk-around we'd do at our own houses this month — an hour outside now saves a midnight phone call later.
articleHtml:
Start where the wind starts: trees and limbs

Walk the yard and look up. Any limb overhanging the roof, deck, or service line is a candidate to come down in a 50-mph gust. Dead wood shows itself now — bare branches while the rest of the tree still holds leaves. Prune what you can reach from the ground; hire an arborist for anything over a ladder's height or near wires. Never touch a limb resting on a power line.

Water goes where you let it

  • Sump pump: pour a bucket of water into the pit. If the float doesn't kick the pump on within seconds, service it now — and remember it dies with the power unless it has battery backup.
  • Gutters and downspouts: clear them, then confirm downspouts discharge at least a few feet from the foundation. Pooling at the sill is how basements flood and framing rots.
  • Garage door: it's the biggest opening in your house. Check that the tracks are tight, rollers move freely, and the bottom seal actually seals — a failed garage door lets wind pressurize the whole structure.

Power and people

Run a portable generator outdoors only, at least 20 feet from the house, exhaust pointed away — never in a garage, even with the door open. Test it under load before the storm, not during. Tape a list by the panel: Eversource outage line, your plumber, and one contractor you trust. We answer 24/7 for storm emergencies across Falmouth, Mashpee, Bourne, and all of Cape Cod. More seasonal checklists are on our blog.
tips: Photograph your roof, siding, and deck now — before-and-after photos make insurance claims far easier.
Charge every drill and headlamp battery when a storm is 48 hours out; they double as phone chargers and light.
If shingles lift at the edges when you press them by hand, the sealant strip has failed — get eyes on the roof before winter.
ctaLine: Not sure whether that leaning oak or soft roof edge can wait until spring? Call Dave at (508) 388-5120 — the look is free, and estimates come back within 24 hours.
